title: Literacy Programs * Hispanic/Latino Community - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Education > Literacy Programs * Hispanic/Latino Community 3 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter African American Community Hispanic/Latino Community No Filter African American Community Hispanic/Latino Community Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF PLAZA DE NUESTRA COMUNIDAD SPRINGFIELD EDUCATION OFFICE BEHAVIORAL HEALTH & EDUCATION SERVICES YOUTH & FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides educational classes, including English as a Second Language (ESL) and literacy classes. After school and summer youth enrichment programs with a focus on connecting with community resources, hearing from presenters on topics of interest, engaging in volunteer service activities, and gaining skills important for the future, such as resume building, scholarship writing, job searching, personal financial management, and navigating the health system. Child care is provided for ESL and literacy classes 1621 Centennial Boulevard Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Varies with a primarily focus is Latino and immigrant population Hours: Classes and youth enrichment: No standard hours Phone: Monday/Wednesday/Friday 8:30am-1:30pm, Tuesday/Thursday 9am-noon (541) 686-8483 2 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOMOS HISPANAS UNIDAS SILVERTON SOMOS HISPANAS UNIDAS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Encourages the Spanish-speaking community to share their culture and diverse backgrounds. Works with Latino parents and students to provide the knowledge and resources needed to graduate from high school and enter college. 512 N 1st Street Silverton, OR 97381 Eligibility: Nuestros Abuelos: Older adults who identify as Hispanic or Latino Juntos: Families who identify as Hispanic or Latino and have a student in grades 8-12 Hours: No standard hours Email (503) 873-7114 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTHERN OREGON HEAD START LISTO FAMILY LITERACY PROGRAM MEDFORD LISTO FAMILY LITERACY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Program that provides adult English as a Second Language (ESL) instruction and adult basic education for parents. Children participate in the program with their parents by attending developmentally appropriate classes and getting homework help and Spanish reading support. 1001 Beall Lane Central Point, OR 97502 Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-15 Hours: Tuesday/Thursday 5:30pm-8pm (541) 734-5150 1049 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PLAZA DE NUESTRA COMUNIDAD YOUTH & FAMILIES SOMOS HISPANAS UNIDAS SILVERTON SOMOS HISPANAS UNIDAS SOUTHERN OREGON HEAD START LISTO FAMILY LITERACY PROGRAM
